items.text:
NOTICE: Changes to the East Campus Shuttle Route
Effective Monday, September 8

Beginning next week, the East Campus shuttle will follow a revised route to reduce travel time during peak hours. Please note the following:

- The shuttle will no longer stop at Maple Court. Riders should use the new stop at Birch Plaza, one block north.
- Morning service now begins at 7:15 AM and runs every twenty minutes until 9:30 AM.
- A valid student ID must be shown when boarding.

The Maple Court stop will be removed permanently on September 8. A printed schedule is posted at each stop, and live arrival times are available through the campus transit portal. Questions may be directed to transit@campus.edu.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where should riders catch the shuttle after the change?

options.A: At the Maple Court stop as usual
options.B: At the new Birch Plaza stop
options.D: At the East Campus parking lot

questions.explanation: The notice tells riders to use the new stop at Birch Plaza, one block north of Maple Court.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about riders who arrive at Maple Court after September 8?

options.A: They will board the shuttle as before.
options.B: They will pay a small boarding fee.
options.C: They will find no shuttle stop there.
options.D: They will wait for a replacement bus.

questions.explanation: The Maple Court stop is removed permanently on September 8, so no shuttle will stop there.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce a new student ID policy
options.B: To advertise a faster campus parking option
options.C: To remind riders to check the transit portal
options.D: To inform riders of a changed shuttle route

questions.explanation: The notice's overall purpose is to inform riders that the East Campus shuttle route is changing.
